name: Attacks & Damage
summary: Your attacks are your weapons — how to hit and how damage is calculated.

sections.heading: Making an attack
paragraphs: To attack, you take the Attack action and strike with a weapon you are proficient with. Roll a d20 and add your ability modifier and your proficiency bonus; if the total meets or beats the target's Armor Class (AC), you hit.

There is no separate list of 'attacks' for a class — a class attacks with the weapons it is proficient with. Each class page lists those under Attacks, and every weapon's full stats are in the Weapons tab.

sections.heading: Which ability?
paragraphs: Melee weapons normally use Strength; finesse and ranged weapons use Dexterity. Some classes change this: the Puppeteer's Strings of Will lets his Control Wires use Charisma instead. A class feature always tells you when a different ability applies.

sections.heading: Damage on a hit
paragraphs: A hit deals the weapon's damage die plus the same ability modifier you used to attack. A Rapier (1d8) with a +3 modifier deals 1d8 + 3.

Class features can add more. The Puppeteer's Puppet's Spite adds an extra Charisma modifier against a strung enemy, so a Control Wires hit — normally 1d4 + Charisma via Strings of Will — becomes 1d4 + Charisma + Charisma on a strung foe.

sections.heading: Where to find damage
paragraphs: Every weapon's damage die, type, properties, and parry profile live on its page in the Weapons tab. Your class page's Attacks section shows the weapons that class actually uses.
